description: Enforce PRD-prefix consistency when the loop spawns fixup tasks via `task-mgr add --stdin`. Loop engine exports the active prefix via `TASK_MGR_ACTIVE_PREFIX`; `resolve_active_prefix` consults the env var first; cross-PRD prefixes in incoming IDs/dependsOn/depended-on-by are rejected. Closes the failure mode where parent tasks can never complete because their dependencies reference unprefixed (or wrong-prefix) sibling task IDs.

criteria: Rust: No warnings in `cargo check` output
Rust: No warnings in `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` output
Rust: Scoped tests pass via `cargo test -p task-mgr <module>` for touched modules
No breaking changes to public CLI flags or JSON schema
Env-var tests save and restore TASK_MGR_ACTIVE_PREFIX (do not leak between tests — see Learning [1685])
globalAcceptanceCriteria.description: These criteria apply to ALL implementation tasks

priorityPhilosophy.description: Hierarchy of what matters most when implementing tasks
hierarchy: 1. PLAN — Anticipate edge cases before coding
2. PHASE 2 FOUNDATION — ~1 day now to save ~2+ weeks later (1:10+ ratio); we are pre-launch, foundations compound
3. FUNCTIONING CODE — Pragmatic, reliable, wired in per plan
4. CORRECTNESS — Compiles, type-checks, scoped tests pass deterministically
5. CODE QUALITY — Clean code, qualityDimensions satisfied, no warnings
principles: Quality dimensions explicit — qualityDimensions on every task tells you what 'good' looks like
Phase 2 foundation — prefer solutions that lay strong post-launch foundations (1:10+ savings ratio)
Edge cases = test cases — every known edge case must have a corresponding test
Scoped per-iteration tests, full suite at REVIEW-001 — REVIEW must leave the trunk green including pre-existing failures
Ship working code with tests to prove it; handle Option/Result explicitly; avoid unwrap() in production

prohibitedOutcomes: Tests that only assert 'no crash' or check type without verifying content
Tests that mirror implementation internals (break when refactoring)
Abstractions with only one concrete use
Error messages that don't identify what went wrong
Catch-all error handlers that swallow context
Silently bypassing prefix scoping when the env var is stale (must hard-error)
Editing AddTaskInput::apply_prefix — Layer 3's job is to reject bad input BEFORE apply_prefix runs

acceptanceCriteria: Positive: SpawnOpts gains `pub active_prefix: Option<&'a str>` field; doc-comment explains its role (loop pins it; subprocess CLI calls read it via the env var to scope auto-prefix).
Positive: spawn_claude sets `TASK_MGR_ACTIVE_PREFIX` only when `active_prefix.is_some()`, in the same block as the existing TASK_MGR_DIR pin.
Positive: Sequential loop call site (engine.rs ~L1830) passes `params.task_prefix` into the new field.
Positive: Parallel-slot loop call site (engine.rs ~L569) passes the slot/wave's task_prefix.
Positive: All other 17+ SpawnOpts construction sites compile by passing `active_prefix: None`.
Negative: When `active_prefix` is None, the child process's environment must NOT contain TASK_MGR_ACTIVE_PREFIX (must not be set to empty string).

Unit test: spawn_claude with active_prefix: Some("abc-") and a CLAUDE_BINARY wrapper script that prints `env | grep TASK_MGR` — wrapper output contains `TASK_MGR_ACTIVE_PREFIX=abc-` (see Learning [1892], [1624] for the wrapper pattern).
Unit test: spawn_claude with active_prefix: None — wrapper output does NOT contain a TASK_MGR_ACTIVE_PREFIX line.
Unit test: const ACTIVE_PREFIX_ENV is exported and equals "TASK_MGR_ACTIVE_PREFIX".

Known-bad: if the implementation pins an empty string when `active_prefix` is Some("") or None-coerced-to-default(""), the env-var-unset test must fail. Add a test that constructs SpawnOpts via `..SpawnOpts::default()` (no explicit active_prefix) and asserts no env var on child.
Failure mode: if cmd.env fails (extremely rare, e.g. invalid char in name), propagate via TaskMgrError — do not panic.
userStories.description:
Add `active_prefix: Option<&'a str>` to `SpawnOpts` in `src/loop_engine/claude.rs`. In `spawn_claude`, set `cmd.env("TASK_MGR_ACTIVE_PREFIX", p)` next to the existing `TASK_MGR_DIR` pin (~L390-393) when `active_prefix.is_some()`. Update every SpawnOpts construction site (19+ across the codebase) to include the field — the two loop call sites pass the iteration's task_prefix, all others pass None.

DO: Set the env var ONLY when Some — when None, do not call `cmd.env` at all (do not pin empty string; let parent env propagate so manual `task-mgr add` invocations from inside a worktree still work).
DO: Wire the two loop sites: `src/loop_engine/engine.rs` parallel-slot SpawnOpts (currently around L569, look for `working_dir: Some(&slot.working_root)`) passes the slot/wave's `task_prefix`; sequential SpawnOpts (around L1830, look for `working_dir: Some(params.project_root)`) passes `params.task_prefix`.
DO: All other SpawnOpts users (curate, learnings ingestion, merge_resolver, prd_reconcile, progress, watchdog, internal claude.rs sites — anything that isn't a loop iteration) pass `active_prefix: None`.
DO NOT: Edit AddTaskInput::apply_prefix — that's Layer 3's territory.
DO NOT: Hardcode the env var name in more than one place — declare it once as `pub(crate) const ACTIVE_PREFIX_ENV: &str = "TASK_MGR_ACTIVE_PREFIX";` (in claude.rs alongside SpawnOpts) and reuse from add.rs in FEAT-002.
DO NOT: Touch SpawnOpts in `merge_resolver.rs` to thread a real prefix — pass `None`. Merge-resolver doesn't create tasks; resolution should remain conservative.

Edge cases:
- `active_prefix: None`: env var must NOT be set on the child (verify with a CLAUDE_BINARY wrapper that prints env)
- `active_prefix: Some("")`: shouldn't happen but treat as None for safety
- Parallel-slot path: each slot must receive the wave's task_prefix (slots within one wave share the same active PRD)

Known-bad to guard against:
- Calling `cmd.env(name, opts.active_prefix.unwrap_or(""))` would set `TASK_MGR_ACTIVE_PREFIX=` (empty) on the child, defeating Layer 2's gate. Use `if let Some(p) = active_prefix` instead.
edgeCases: active_prefix: None → env var must not appear in child env
active_prefix: Some("abc-") → env var TASK_MGR_ACTIVE_PREFIX=abc- in child env
Parallel slot: each slot receives the wave's prefix (slots share same PRD)
merge_resolver path: keep None (does not create tasks)

failureModes.cause: Loop call site forgets to thread params.task_prefix
failureModes.expectedBehavior: Compile-time visible; field is mandatory in struct literal (no default fallback at non-Default sites)

userStories.notes: SpawnOpts is `#[derive(Default)]` (see claude.rs:89) so call sites that use `..SpawnOpts::default()` need no edit. But all 19+ existing sites use full struct literal syntax — each gets `active_prefix: None,`. Use `grep -rn 'SpawnOpts {' src/` to enumerate. Loop call sites: engine.rs:~569 (slot.working_root path = parallel) and engine.rs:~1830 (project_root path = sequential). Keep `merge_resolver.rs:~252` at None per plan (out of scope). Learning [1828]: SpawnOpts is the clean abstraction; just add the field. Learning [1892]/[1624]: use CLAUDE_BINARY wrapper for env-var verification.
qualityDimensions: All SpawnOpts call sites compile and pass None when not a loop iteration
Env var pinned only when Some — never empty string, never on None path
Single source for env var name (constant, not string literal repeated)
Idiomatic Rust: explicit `if let Some(p)` instead of unwrap_or-default
Test isolation: subprocess env-var test does not leak into the test process's env
userStories.title: Pin TASK_MGR_ACTIVE_PREFIX in spawn_claude; thread active_prefix through SpawnOpts

acceptanceCriteria: Positive: resolve_active_prefix returns `TaskMgrResult<Option<String>>` (signature change documented in rustdoc).
Positive: When TASK_MGR_ACTIVE_PREFIX is set and present in prd_metadata, returns Ok(Some(env_value)).
Positive: When env var unset, falls back to existing 'exactly one non-NULL prefix' logic — Ok(Some(prefix)) or Ok(None).
Positive: When env var set to empty string, treated as unset (Ok(...) per fallback, not Err).
Negative: When env var set to a value NOT present in prd_metadata, returns Err with both the stale value and the list of known prefixes in the message.
Negative: Function does not call std::env::var more than once per invocation.
Unit test: env unset, single-PRD with prefix 'A-' → Ok(Some("A-")) (regression: existing behavior).
Unit test: env unset, two PRDs ('A-', 'B-') → Ok(None) (regression: existing behavior).
Unit test: env="A-", prd_metadata has 'A-' and 'B-' → Ok(Some("A-")).
Unit test: env="stale-", prd_metadata has 'A-' only → Err; error message contains "stale-" and "A-".
Unit test: env="", multi-PRD → Ok(None) (empty treated as unset).
Unit test: prd_metadata empty, env="X-" set → Err (still stale; no prefix is valid).
Known-bad: a naive impl that returns Ok(Some(env)) without verifying must fail the env="stale-" test.
CONTRACT: ACTIVE_PREFIX_ENV constant is the only string-literal source of "TASK_MGR_ACTIVE_PREFIX" — grep src/ to verify no second occurrence.
Caller updated: add_with_conn at the resolve_active_prefix call site uses `?` to propagate the Result.
Failure mode: if prd_metadata query itself fails (DB error), bubble via `?` — do not coerce to None.
Test isolation: every env-var test saves the prior value and restores it in a guard / defer (Learning [1685] — clap env shadowing). Use a small RAII helper or `scopeguard`-style closure.
userStories.description:
Refactor `resolve_active_prefix` in `src/commands/add.rs` (currently L383-397, returns `Option<String>`) to consult `TASK_MGR_ACTIVE_PREFIX` first. Change return type to `TaskMgrResult<Option<String>>` so a stale env var (set but not present in `prd_metadata`) can hard-error. Update the single caller in `add_with_conn` (L232) to handle the Result via `?`.

DO: Resolution order — (1) read env var; if set non-empty, query prd_metadata for that exact prefix; if found → Ok(Some(value)); if NOT found → Err(invalid_state). (2) Env var unset or empty → existing fallback (return Ok(Some) only when exactly one non-NULL prefix exists, else Ok(None)).
DO: Reuse the env var name constant from FEAT-001 (`claude::ACTIVE_PREFIX_ENV` or equivalent path) — do not hardcode the string in add.rs.
DO: Make the error message name BOTH the offending env value and the list of known prefixes, so an operator can immediately spot the typo.
DO NOT: Reach into the loop engine's internals — only the env-var name constant should cross the module boundary.
DO NOT: Call std::env::var inside a hot loop — read once at function entry.

BEHAVIOR CHANGE — caller impact:
- `add_with_conn` (single caller) must change `let resolved_prefix = resolve_active_prefix(conn);` to `let resolved_prefix = resolve_active_prefix(conn)?;`. Propagation is straightforward — the surrounding function already returns TaskMgrResult.
- No other callers exist (it's a private fn). Verified via `grep -rn 'resolve_active_prefix' src/`.

Edge cases:
- Env var unset: Ok(None) when multi-PRD, Ok(Some) when single-PRD (existing behavior preserved)
- Env var = "": treat as unset (do not error)
- Env var = "abc-" and prd_metadata has 'abc-' → Ok(Some("abc-"))
- Env var = "stale-" not in prd_metadata → Err with both the stale value and list of known prefixes
- prd_metadata empty (no PRDs) but env var set → Err (still stale)

Known-bad to guard against:
- A naive impl returning Ok(Some(env_value)) without verifying it exists in prd_metadata defeats the safety check entirely. Test must seed prd_metadata with prefix 'A-', set env var to 'B-', and assert Err.
edgeCases: Env unset → existing single/multi-PRD fallback unchanged
Env empty string → treat as unset, no error
Env value present in prd_metadata → use it
Env value absent from prd_metadata → hard error with diagnostic context
prd_metadata empty + env set → error (no valid prefix exists)
DB query failure during prefix verification → propagate, not coerce

failureModes.expectedBehavior: Err with message naming the bad value and known prefixes
Propagate as DB error via `?`; do not silently degrade to None

userStories.notes: Single caller: add_with_conn at src/commands/add.rs:232. Existing fallback already uses `prepare("SELECT task_prefix FROM prd_metadata WHERE task_prefix IS NOT NULL")`. For the env-var path, do an additional verification query: `SELECT 1 FROM prd_metadata WHERE task_prefix = ? LIMIT 1`. Reuse Learning [538]: tests that seed prd_metadata must include the task_prefix column. Reuse Learning [516]: migration v9 schema. Use TaskMgrError::invalid_state with field="TASK_MGR_ACTIVE_PREFIX". Reach for a tiny env-var test guard pattern (save/restore in Drop) to avoid Learning [1685]'s clap env shadowing class of bug.
qualityDimensions: Hard-fail on stale env var rather than silent fallback — surfaces config drift loudly
Single std::env::var call per invocation
Error message names BOTH the bad value and the legal alternatives
Empty string treated identically to unset (defensive against shell quoting bugs)
Env-var tests do not leak state between tests (RAII restore)
Caller propagation via `?` — no .ok() / .unwrap_or(None) which would defeat the hard-error
userStories.title: Make resolve_active_prefix env-var-aware; hard-error on stale value

acceptanceCriteria: Positive: New helper `reject_foreign_prefix(conn, id, depends_on, depended_on_by, active_prefix) -> TaskMgrResult<()>` lives in add.rs.
Positive: Called in add_with_conn after resolve_active_prefix returns Some(active) and before input.apply_prefix.
Positive: Uses `format!("{prefix}-")` (with trailing dash) for the starts_with check — trailing dash is mandatory.
Positive: Error message includes the offending field name (id / dependsOn / depended-on-by), the offending ID, the foreign prefix, the active prefix, and a 'pass --from-json or correct the ID' hint.
Negative: Does NOT reject bare IDs (no recognizable prefix) — those flow through to apply_prefix.
Negative: Does NOT reject already-active-prefixed IDs (idempotent path).
Negative: Does NOT modify AddTaskInput::apply_prefix or super::init::prefix_id.
Unit test: active 'A-', input 'A-FEAT-001' → Ok (idempotent).
Unit test: active 'A-', input 'FEAT-001' → Ok (bare).
Unit test: active 'A-', input 'B-FEAT-001' (B- in metadata) → Err with all 5 message components.
Unit test: active 'A-', dependsOn=['B-FEAT-1'] → Err naming the field as 'dependsOn'.
Unit test: active 'A-', depended_on_by=['B-FEAT-1'] → Err naming the field as 'depended-on-by'.
Unit test: active 'AB-', input 'A-FEAT-001' (both AB- and A- in metadata) → Err (foreign 'A-' detected via trailing-dash check).
Unit test: active 'A-', input 'AB-FEAT-001' (both A- and AB- in metadata) → Ok against 'A-' (because 'AB-FEAT-001'.starts_with('A-') is FALSE) — this is the discriminator.
Unit test: prd_metadata has only the active prefix → no rejection possible, all inputs pass.

Unit test: integration via add_with_conn — bare ID + bare dependsOn under env var 'A-' produces a row with id='A-FIX-001' and dependsOn=['A-OTHER-1'] (verifies the FEAT-002 + FEAT-003 + apply_prefix chain end-to-end).
Known-bad: a naive `id.starts_with(foreign)` (no trailing dash) must FAIL the AB-/A- discriminator test.
Failure mode: if prd_metadata query fails, propagate via `?`; do not silently allow.
userStories.description:
Add a private helper `reject_foreign_prefix` in `src/commands/add.rs` that runs in `add_with_conn` AFTER `resolve_active_prefix` returns Some(active) and BEFORE `input.apply_prefix(active)` (currently L236). Rejects when `input.id`, any `input.depends_on` entry, or any `--depended-on-by` target carries a known PRD prefix that is NOT the active one.

DO: Query all non-NULL `task_prefix` values from prd_metadata once. Strip the active prefix from the set. For each remaining 'foreign' prefix, check whether any of the three input fields starts with `format!("{foreign}-")` — note the trailing dash, otherwise prefix 'A' false-matches 'AB-FEAT-001'.
DO: Build the error message to name (a) the offending field, (b) the offending ID, (c) the foreign prefix detected, (d) the active prefix, (e) actionable guidance: 'pass --from-json or correct the ID'.
DO: Apply this check to BOTH input.depends_on and the depended_on_by Vec passed to add_with_conn.
DO NOT: Modify AddTaskInput::apply_prefix or the prefix_id helper — they're load-bearing and idempotent. The check is purely a gate before them.
DO NOT: Reject IDs that have NO recognizable prefix (bare like 'FIX-001') — those are fine, apply_prefix will handle them.
DO NOT: Reject IDs that already start with the ACTIVE prefix — idempotent path, must pass through.

Edge cases:
- Active 'A-', input 'A-FEAT-001' → OK (idempotent)
- Active 'A-', input 'FEAT-001' → OK (bare, will be auto-prefixed by apply_prefix)
- Active 'A-', input 'B-FEAT-001' (B- exists in prd_metadata) → ERROR
- Active 'A-', input 'X-FEAT-001' (X- not in any prd_metadata row) → OK (unknown prefix, will be left alone or accepted at agent's risk — apply_prefix is idempotent only against the active prefix anyway, so this becomes 'A-X-FEAT-001' after auto-prefix; document this corner case)
- Active 'A-', depends_on=['B-FEAT-1'] (B- in metadata) → ERROR
- Active 'A-', depends_on=['A-OTHER-1'] → OK
- Active 'AB-', input 'A-FEAT-001' (A- in metadata) → ERROR (A- is foreign even though AB- starts with A; trailing-dash check ensures 'A-' matches as A-, not AB-)
- prd_metadata has only the active prefix → no foreign rejection possible; OK to pass through

Known-bad to guard against:
- A naive `id.starts_with(foreign)` (no trailing dash) would false-match: input='A-FEAT-001' against foreign='AB' would pass, but input='AB-FEAT-001' against foreign='A' would also pass and incorrectly reject. Test must include sibling prefixes that share leading characters.
edgeCases: Idempotent: input already starts with active prefix → OK
Bare: input has no recognizable prefix → OK (apply_prefix handles)
Foreign prefix in input.id → ERROR
Foreign prefix in dependsOn → ERROR
Foreign prefix in depended_on_by → ERROR
Sibling prefixes that share leading chars (A- vs AB-): trailing-dash check disambiguates
prd_metadata has only the active prefix → no foreign exists to reject
Unknown (not-in-metadata) prefix in input → not rejected (apply_prefix will prepend active, yielding A-X-FEAT-001 — not ideal but not silently wrong)

failureModes.expectedBehavior: Hard error with field name, value, foreign prefix, active prefix, and 'pass --from-json or correct the ID' hint

userStories.notes: Place the helper next to resolve_active_prefix at the bottom of add.rs. Use the same prepared-statement style as the existing function. The integration test that covers FEAT-002 + FEAT-003 + apply_prefix together is the most valuable single test in this PRD — it proves the original bug is fixed end-to-end. Pattern reuse: `super::init::prefix_id` is already used by apply_prefix; do NOT reimplement prefix logic. Learning [2281]/[2236]: this is exactly the bug class being defended against — a misfiled spawn-fixup. CONTRACT: this is a write-side gate; the read-side dispatch (detection.rs, complete.rs) is intentionally NOT changed (see plan Non-goals).
qualityDimensions: Trailing-dash check on every prefix comparison — never bare starts_with
Error messages name field, value, foreign prefix, active prefix, and remediation
Helper is private — single call site, no external API surface added
No mutation in the helper; pure validation that returns Ok/Err
Single prd_metadata query — collect once, iterate in memory
Idempotent path (already-active-prefixed) and bare path both explicitly covered by tests
userStories.title: Reject foreign-prefix IDs in add_with_conn before apply_prefix runs

acceptanceCriteria: Positive: task_ops.rs has a new sentence/paragraph at an appropriate spot describing the auto-prefix + cross-PRD rejection behavior.
Positive: CLAUDE.md 'Spawn-fixup PRD targeting' bullet has a new note (prepended or appended) describing TASK_MGR_ACTIVE_PREFIX and the auto-prefix safety net.
Positive: Existing `--from-json` / `--depended-on-by` advice in CLAUDE.md is preserved verbatim.
Positive: Existing real-incident example in CLAUDE.md ('loop-reliability.json' callout) is preserved verbatim.
Negative: No other prompt_sections files touched.
Negative: No new top-level CLAUDE.md section added — extend existing.
Verification: `grep -n 'TASK_MGR_ACTIVE_PREFIX' CLAUDE.md src/loop_engine/prompt_sections/task_ops.rs` returns at least one match in each file.
Verification: `grep -c 'Spawn-fixup PRD targeting' CLAUDE.md` returns 1 (no duplicate section).
userStories.description:
Single doc-update task for both layers of teaching:

1. `src/loop_engine/prompt_sections/task_ops.rs`: append one sentence to the existing task_ops section saying 'task-mgr add --stdin auto-prefixes IDs and dependsOn to the active PRD when the loop is running; you may write IDs as bare (FIX-…-001) or fully prefixed and both work. Cross-PRD IDs (those starting with another PRD's prefix) are rejected.'
2. `CLAUDE.md` (root): update the 'Spawn-fixup PRD targeting' bullet under '3a · task-mgr Workflow Patterns'. Keep the existing `--from-json` / `--depended-on-by` advice as best-practice (still useful for human invocations) but prefix the section with a note that the loop now exports TASK_MGR_ACTIVE_PREFIX and the CLI auto-prefixes new tasks + their deps + their depended-on-by targets. Mention the cross-PRD hard-error.

DO: Keep the existing CLAUDE.md text — append/prepend, don't rewrite. Future readers benefit from the historical context (the 'real example' callout about loop-reliability.json is valuable Learning [2281] context).
DO: Use the prompt template's existing tone — short, actionable, no fluff.
DO NOT: Touch src/loop_engine/prompt_sections/*.rs files other than task_ops.rs.
DO NOT: Add a new CLAUDE.md section — extend the existing one.
DO NOT: Modify the loop-reliability.json incident example — it's a real artifact.

Edge cases:
- task_ops.rs is included verbatim in the prompt template — wording must compose with the surrounding text without orphan paragraphs.
- CLAUDE.md is read by humans AND by tests (e.g., `tests/no_hardcoded_models.rs` does NOT scan it, but other CLAUDE.md tests may exist) — verify no test fixture greps for an exact string we're about to reword.

Known-bad to guard against:
- Replacing the existing best-practice advice would lose context for human invocations of `task-mgr add` outside a loop. The CLI's auto-prefix only fires when env var is set OR exactly one PRD exists; humans on a multi-PRD DB without the env var still need the old advice.
edgeCases: task_ops.rs is part of the rendered prompt — wording must read cleanly as a continuation of the existing section
CLAUDE.md may be referenced by other tooling that greps for headers — preserve header text

failureModes.cause: Existing CLAUDE.md grep-target text accidentally rewritten
failureModes.expectedBehavior: Caught by REVIEW-001's grep verification step before merge

userStories.notes: task_ops.rs lives at src/loop_engine/prompt_sections/task_ops.rs (currently L1-37). The existing teaching at L27-34 is the natural anchor — add the new sentence right after the `--depended-on-by` bullet. CLAUDE.md '3a · task-mgr Workflow Patterns' section contains the 'Spawn-fixup PRD targeting' bullet — add the new note as the first sentence so it's the first thing readers see, then 'Two reliable forms: (a) ... (b) ...' continues as before.
qualityDimensions: Existing context preserved verbatim (real-incident callouts retained)
New text composes naturally with surrounding prose — no orphan paragraphs
Single point of teaching update — task_ops.rs + CLAUDE.md, nothing else
userStories.title: Update prompt teaching + CLAUDE.md to reflect automatic prefix enforcement

acceptanceCriteria: Single source for the env var name string (constant); grep src/ confirms one definition
No duplicate prd_metadata queries — if FEAT-002 and FEAT-003 both load the prefix set in add_with_conn, hoist into one query
reject_foreign_prefix and resolve_active_prefix are both private to add.rs (not pub)
Functions stay under 30 lines each
Pattern consistency with existing add.rs helpers (resolve_priority, locate_prd_json)
Any issues found spawn REFACTOR-FIX-xxx tasks via `task-mgr add --stdin --depended-on-by REVIEW-001`
userStories.description:
Review all FEAT-001..FEAT-004 implementation for DRY violations, complexity, coupling, and pattern adherence.

DO: Verify the env var name is declared once (constant) and reused — both spawn_claude pin and resolve_active_prefix consult the same constant.
DO: Verify reject_foreign_prefix is a single coherent helper, not split across multiple files.
DO: Verify no duplicated prd_metadata.task_prefix queries — if FEAT-002 and FEAT-003 each query prd_metadata, consider consolidating into one query that returns the full prefix set.
DO NOT: Skip — the env var name has 4 natural references (claude.rs constant, spawn_claude pin, add.rs read, tests) and stringly-typed drift is a real risk.
userStories.notes: If issues found: `echo '{...}' | task-mgr add --stdin --depended-on-by REVIEW-001` for each (priority 50-97) — atomic DB+JSON sync, no manual JSON edit. If no issues, invoke /simplify on any ugly touchpoint, then emit `<task-status>REFACTOR-001:done</task-status>` with a one-line progress note.
qualityDimensions: DRY across modules (env var name, prd_metadata queries)
Single-responsibility functions
Pattern consistency with existing add.rs helpers
userStories.title: Review for refactoring opportunities

acceptanceCriteria: Positive: All new code reachable from production entry points (grep-verified — both loop call sites in engine.rs thread the prefix; spawn_claude pins the env var; resolve_active_prefix reads it).
Positive: All errors propagated with context (?, map_err) — no unwrap in production paths.
Positive: FULL `cargo test` (no scope), `cargo clippy -- -D warnings`, `cargo fmt --check` all clean.
Negative: No unwrap() in production code paths added by this PRD.
Negative: No new dead code warnings.
Integration: synthetic two-PRD DB + env-var-set spawn produces a row with the active prefix on ID + dependsOn + depended-on-by; foreign-prefix input rejected.
Documentation: task_ops.rs + CLAUDE.md updates verified by grep (TASK_MGR_ACTIVE_PREFIX appears in both).
Task update: any FEAT task descriptions changed by the implementation (API changes, signature drift) are reflected in the JSON via `task-mgr init --from-json --append --update-existing`.
Pre-existing test failures fixed (or spawned as FIX-xxx with verifyCommand if >~12 unrelated).
If issues found: FIX-xxx tasks spawned via `task-mgr add --stdin --depended-on-by REVIEW-001`.
userStories.description:
Review all implementation for quality, security, integration wiring, and documentation. RUN THE FULL UNSCOPED QUALITY GATE.

DO: Verify spawn_claude actually pins the env var at runtime — write or run an integration test with a CLAUDE_BINARY wrapper that prints env, confirm TASK_MGR_ACTIVE_PREFIX appears with the expected value during a sequential loop iteration AND a parallel-slot iteration.
DO: Verify reject_foreign_prefix actually fires for the documented bug class — reproduce with a synthetic two-PRD DB.
DO: Run full `cargo test` (no -p filter) and fix every failure including pre-existing.
DO: Verify CLAUDE.md and task_ops.rs updates are coherent; the 'Spawn-fixup PRD targeting' grep-target header is intact.
DO NOT: Skip wiring verification — the #1 failure mode is the env var being set in SpawnOpts but not actually reaching the child process due to a missed call site.
userStories.notes: Spawn fixes via `echo '{...}' | task-mgr add --stdin --depended-on-by REVIEW-001`. If clean review: emit `<task-status>REVIEW-001:done</task-status>` with 'Clean review' note. The most valuable test in this PRD is an end-to-end one: synthetic two-PRD DB, set TASK_MGR_ACTIVE_PREFIX=A-, call task-mgr add with a bare ID and a bare dependsOn, verify the resulting row has id=A-FIX-001 + dependsOn=[A-OTHER-1] in the DB. Then repeat with a B-prefixed input ID and verify Err. Belt-and-suspenders: verify the original failure mode (the user's reported `bae3f9de-REVIEW-001` ↔ `FIX-PREEXISTING-INTEGRATION-001` shape) cannot reproduce in a fresh loop run.
qualityDimensions: No unwrap in production
All new code wired to production entry points (env var actually reaches child process)
Full suite green including pre-existing
End-to-end integration test proves the original bug class is fixed
Documentation updates grep-verified
userStories.title: Code review + final verification

acceptanceCriteria: Single private helper loads the prefix list; the literal SQL 'FROM prd_metadata WHERE task_prefix IS NOT NULL' appears at most once in src/commands/add.rs
reject_foreign_prefix function body under 30 lines
resolve_active_prefix function body under 30 lines
All 34 existing commands::add tests still pass
cargo clippy -- -D warnings clean
Helpers remain private (not pub)
userStories.description: REFACTOR-001 found DRY violations and length overruns in src/commands/add.rs. (1) The query 'SELECT task_prefix FROM prd_metadata WHERE task_prefix IS NOT NULL' repeats 3x at lines 416, 436, 467 across resolve_active_prefix (stale-error and fallback paths) and reject_foreign_prefix. Hoist to a private helper load_known_prefixes(conn) -> TaskMgrResult<Vec<String>>. (2) reject_foreign_prefix is 62 lines with three near-identical TaskMgrError::invalid_state blocks differing only in the field name; extract an inner closure or helper. (3) resolve_active_prefix is 51 lines and shrinks naturally once the duplicated query is hoisted. Preserve all behavior: 34 existing commands::add tests must pass; helpers stay private; public signatures unchanged.
userStories.title: Consolidate prd_metadata prefix queries + shrink resolve_active_prefix/reject_foreign_prefix
